title: Worldwide Immigration Trends Reports | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Worldwide Immigration Trends Report: Challenges and Opportunities for 2026
Global immigration is continuing to transform at lightning speed, further fueled by geopolitical
                    uncertainty, tariff and regulatory shifts and heightened compliance. When the only constant is
                    change and the risks need to be carefully weighed against the gains, taking a proactive, nimble
                    approach is key to successful mobility planning.
As your organization strategizes for attracting and safeguarding international talent in 2026 and
                    beyond, here are three key areas that deserve your focus.

## Sourcing talent in a world of disruption and new talent hubs
Companies are shifting from a reactive approach to a proactive one, increasingly focusing
                      on
                      diversifying and resilience-proofing their workforce strategies. As they navigate
                      regional
                      instability, tap into emerging business hubs and respond to rapidly evolving
                      immigration
                      regulations, they are redefining the planning and deployment of talent globally.

## Redefining value in global mobility amid talent shortages and high costs
With nearly 75% of employers worldwide struggling to find the talent they need, many are
                      turning to the global talent pool. Rising costs are prompting organizations to
                      reevaluate
                      the ROI of their mobility strategy for both short- and long-term growth. Though
                      challenging,
                      these global assignments can be central to developing talent, transferring knowledge and
                      driving organizational success.

## Navigating the intensifying global compliance landscape
Companies are increasingly facing heightened government enforcement as a result of
                      rapidly
                      advancing technologies and a renewed emphasis on domestic talent. While this complicates
                      mobility planning, advancements are also optimizing processes, enabling organizations to
                      accelerate application timelines and strengthen their overall compliance readiness.
The “ Worldwide Immigration Trends Report: Challenges and Opportunities for 2026 ”
                    leverages decades of experience and a global perspective to help you tackle these key challenges
                    and make smart decisions.
